citations.citation_title: Obesity and Weight Management for the Prevention and Treatment of Type 2 Diabetes
citations.guideline_source: American Diabetes Association (ADA) 2024 Standards of Care in Diabetes
citations.quote: In adults with type 2 diabetes and overweight or obesity, pharmacologic therapy for weight management is recommended as adjunct to lifestyle intervention, with preference for agents such as semaglutide or tirzepatide that provide substantial weight loss and glycemic benefit.
citations.supports_claim: GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy (semaglutide/Ozempic) is a guideline-recommended treatment for patients with type 2 diabetes and BMI >= 30.

citations.citation_title: AACE Clinical Practice Guideline for the Medical Care of Patients with Obesity
citations.guideline_source: American Association of Clinical Endocrinology (AACE) 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline on Obesity
citations.quote: Pharmacotherapy for obesity is recommended as an adjunct to lifestyle therapy for patients with BMI >= 30, or >= 27 with weight-related comorbidities, to improve weight loss and reduce cardiometabolic risk.
citations.supports_claim: BMI >= 30 qualifies the patient for anti-obesity pharmacotherapy including GLP-1 receptor agonists.

citations.guideline_source: American Heart Association/American College of Cardiology/The Obesity Society (AHA/ACC/TOS) 2013 Guideline for the Management of Overweight and Obesity in Adults
citations.quote: Adults with BMI >= 30 should be offered comprehensive lifestyle intervention and may be considered for adjunctive pharmacotherapy when lifestyle measures alone are insufficient to achieve weight-loss goals.
citations.supports_claim: BMI >= 30 meets guideline thresholds for obesity pharmacotherapy in addition to diet and exercise.
